topic: Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers
subtopic: Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha, President, membership, quorum, summoning, adjournment, prorogation and officers

content.issueStory: Parliamentary procedure turns representation into accountable law-making; confusing adjournment, prorogation and dissolution or House-specific offices creates predictable prelims errors. Parliament consists of President, Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha. The Houses differ in tenure, composition and special powers. Sessions are summoned and prorogued by the President, while presiding officers manage sittings and procedure under constitutional and House rules.
content.coreConcept: Parliament consists of President, Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha. The Houses differ in tenure, composition and special powers. Sessions are summoned and prorogued by the President, while presiding officers manage sittings and procedure under constitutional and House rules.
dimensions: Rajya Sabha is continuing, with periodic retirement; Lok Sabha is normally term-bound and dissolvable.
Representation and nomination rules differ between the Houses.
Summoning, prorogation, adjournment and dissolution are distinct acts by different authorities.
Constitution sets a maximum interval between sessions rather than a fixed minimum number of annual sessions.
Speaker, Deputy Speaker, Chairman and Deputy Chairman differ in source, vote and removal.
Quorum, vacancies, oath and disqualification rules preserve valid House functioning.
content.dataReport: Uploaded source basis: STATIC_09_Polity.pdf and PYQ_06_Polity_Governance.pdf. Refresh amendments, statutory rules, office status and later judgments from official sources: Constitution of India, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha procedure portals.
content.caseStudy: Adjournment ends or pauses a sitting by presiding authority, while prorogation ends a session through the President; neither is the same as Lok Sabha dissolution.
argumentsFor: Bicameralism adds federal and revising roles.
argumentsAgainst: Disruption displaces committee and floor work.
content.pyqLink: UPSC patterns test Article-institution matches, exceptions, procedure and comparative statements within Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers. Resolve each statement through source, scope, procedure and consequence.

answerFramework.intro: Introduce Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers through the controlling constitutional principle and legal source.
body: Map the authority: Articles 79-104, 85, 89-98 and House rules.

Explain the first-order design: Rajya Sabha is continuing, with periodic retirement; Lok Sabha is normally term-bound and dissolvable. Representation and nomination rules differ between the Houses. Summoning, prorogation, adjournment and dissolution are distinct acts by different authorities.

Analyse safeguards and tensions: Constitution sets a maximum interval between sessions rather than a fixed minimum number of annual sessions. Speaker, Deputy Speaker, Chairman and Deputy Chairman differ in source, vote and removal. Quorum, vacancies, oath and disqualification rules preserve valid House functioning.

Use Adjournment ends or pauses a sitting by presiding authority, while prorogation ends a session through the President; neither is the same as Lok Sabha dissolution. and close with constitutional accountability plus dated legal verification.
answerFramework.conclusion: A strong Polity answer joins constitutional text, institutional purpose, judicial doctrine and a workable accountability safeguard.

mainsExamples: Rajya Sabha continuing despite Lok Sabha dissolution.
Speaker certifying procedural matters under law.
prelimsFacts: President is a constituent part of Parliament.
Vice-President is ex officio Rajya Sabha Chairman.
Speaker normally has a casting vote under equality rules.
Six months must not intervene between last sitting and first sitting of next session.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the controlling rule in Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers?
revisionBox.answer: Parliament consists of President, Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha. The Houses differ in tenure, composition and special powers. Sessions are summoned and prorogued by the President, while presiding officers manage sittings and procedure under constitutional and House rules.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the primary constitutional or legal authority?
What is the fastest elimination trap?
revisionBox.answer: Do not call adjournment a presidential act.

revisionBox.prompt: Where should later legal changes be verified?
revisionBox.answer: Constitution of India, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha procedure portals

mindmap_json.center: Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers

branches.label: Text
children: Rajya Sabha is continuing, with periodic retirement; Lok Sabha is normally term-bound and dissolvable.
Representation and nomination rules differ between the Houses.

branches.label: Institution
children: Summoning, prorogation, adjournment and dissolution are distinct acts by different authorities.
Constitution sets a maximum interval between sessions rather than a fixed minimum number of annual sessions.

branches.label: Safeguard

Quorum, vacancies, oath and disqualification rules preserve valid House functioning.

children: Do not call adjournment a presidential act.
Do not equate prorogation with dissolution.

Vice-President is ex officio Rajya Sabha Chairman.
Speaker normally has a casting vote under equality rules.
Six months must not intervene between last sitting and first sitting of next session.

Do not equate prorogation with dissolution.

pyq_refs.question: PYQ pattern: constitutional statement testing on Parliament: Composition, Sessions and Presiding Officers.
pyq_refs.answer: Check text, scope, exception and competent authority.
